labels.name: type:test
labels.description: Verification, replay, or test harness work

labels.name: area:brand
labels.description: Brand, naming, and visual system work

milestones.title: M0 Foundation
milestones.description: Definition of Done: repository scaffold, GitHub operating system, branding seed, naming system, and CI guardrails are live.

milestones.title: M1 Kernel v1
milestones.description: Definition of Done: canonical domain model, runtime state machine, event schema, replay semantics, and golden trace harness are specified.

milestones.title: M2 GitHub Backbone
milestones.description: Definition of Done: GitHub mapping, sync contracts, reconciliation model, and drift rules are explicit and reviewable.

milestones.title: M3 Orchestration
milestones.description: Definition of Done: company model, planner, scheduler, and approval semantics are simulable on paper without ambiguity.

milestones.title: M4 Execution Plane
milestones.description: Definition of Done: execution packet, task-result contract, effect envelopes, policy model, and artifact storage rules are fail-closed.

milestones.title: M5 Memory
milestones.description: Definition of Done: memory strata, extraction, retrieval, provenance, and evaluation rules are defined.

milestones.title: M6 Control Plane
milestones.description: Definition of Done: public API, operator timeline, control endpoints, onboarding, and GitHub-linked progress views are designed.

milestones.title: M7 Alpha Readiness
milestones.description: Definition of Done: observability, replay regression, security review, reference workflows, and release runbook are ready for alpha.

issues.title: Define the product charter, MVP scope, and glossary
issues.body:
Outcome:
Produce the founding product charter for Agents Company, including mission, MVP scope boundaries, non-goals, and shared glossary.

Validation:
A single document defines the product north star, in-scope MVP surfaces, out-of-scope items, and common terminology without unresolved contradictions.

issues.title: Define the clean-room policy and no-copy operating rule
issues.body:
Outcome:
Write the clean-room build policy and provenance checklist for this repository.

Validation:
Contributors can tell what kinds of external inspiration are allowed, what is forbidden, and what proof of originality must accompany future implementation work.

issues.title: Design the monorepo topology and bounded contexts
issues.body:
Outcome:
Define the intended package and application boundaries for the new monorepo, including ownership seams between core, UI, runtime, API, and future apps.

Validation:
The topology can be implemented without renaming or reworking the same boundary twice.

issues.title: Design the GitHub-first operating system
issues.body:
Outcome:
Define labels, milestones, issue and PR templates, workflow states, and the rule set for how humans and subagents operate through GitHub.

Validation:
A contributor can join the project and understand how work enters, advances, blocks, and closes through GitHub alone.

issues.title: Define CI baseline, branch protection, and CODEOWNERS
issues.body:
Outcome:
Establish the baseline repository controls for merge safety, review discipline, and required checks.

Validation:
The repository enforces PR-based delivery with at least one required check and clear ownership for all paths.

issues.title: Create the brand guide from the approved logo seed
issues.body:
Outcome:
Turn the approved logo seed into the first official brand guide covering naming, palette, usage rules, and pending brand assets.

Validation:
Future product surfaces can reference one documented brand source instead of improvising style decisions.

issues.title: Define the canonical technical naming system
issues.body:
Outcome:
Lock the repository slug, package scopes, Go module path, CLI name, app identifiers, and storage or environment prefixes.

Validation:
There is one authoritative naming map for all technical surfaces and future refactors do not have to guess names.

issues.title: Define the canonical domain model
issues.body:
Outcome:
Define the domain entities for company, agent, objective, work item, run, approval, artifact, and related invariants.

Validation:
The model supports deterministic execution planning without vague or overlapping concepts.

issues.title: Define the runtime state machine and event schema
issues.body:
Outcome:
Specify the allowed runtime states, transitions, event types, and event payload expectations.

Validation:
Every runtime transition can be replayed and explained from typed events rather than inferred from side effects.

issues.title: Design replay, idempotency, and retry semantics
issues.body:
Outcome:
Define how the kernel replays runs, detects duplicates, and handles retries without breaking determinism.

Validation:
The project has explicit rules for replay safety, duplicate suppression, and retry boundaries.

issues.title: Design the persistent ledger and materialized projections
issues.body:
Outcome:
Define the storage model for the internal ledger and the projections needed by operator-facing surfaces.

Validation:
The ledger truth and the projected read models are separated clearly and can evolve independently.

issues.title: Design the golden trace harness
issues.body:
Outcome:
Define the contract-test harness that uses golden traces to validate reducer behavior and replay stability.

Validation:
A future implementation team has a clear verification path for correctness against canonical traces.

issues.title: Design the kernel-to-GitHub mapping
issues.body:
Outcome:
Define how objectives, work items, approvals, and runs project into GitHub issues, milestones, comments, checks, and metadata.

Validation:
The mapping preserves human clarity without turning GitHub into runtime truth.

issues.title: Design GitHub App authentication and permissions
issues.body:
Outcome:
Define the GitHub App installation flow, token model, repository permissions, and minimum scopes required.

Validation:
The integration can operate safely with least privilege and without permission ambiguity.

issues.title: Design outbound GitHub synchronization
issues.body:
Outcome:
Define how runtime progress is projected outward into GitHub updates, comments, and checks.

Validation:
Outbound sync behavior is deterministic, observable, and bounded by explicit rules.

issues.title: Design inbound reconciliation and conflict handling
issues.body:
Outcome:
Define how human edits or GitHub changes are interpreted, validated, and reconciled against the internal ledger.

Validation:
The project has a conflict policy that prevents silent state corruption.

issues.title: Design drift detection and audit rules
issues.body:
Outcome:
Define the audit trail and drift model between the runtime ledger and GitHub projections.

Validation:
A human operator can understand when GitHub and runtime views diverge and how the system responds.

issues.title: Define the organizational model and handoff contracts
issues.body:
Outcome:
Define the company-of-agents structure, role boundaries, and allowed handoff semantics.

Validation:
The orchestration layer can allocate work without relying on conversational ambiguity.

issues.title: Design the planner/compiler
issues.body:
Outcome:
Define how a high-level objective becomes an executable plan graph with bounded work items.

Validation:
The planner output is structured, reviewable, and suitable for deterministic scheduling.

issues.title: Design the scheduler with leases and retries
issues.body:
Outcome:
Define concurrency caps, leases, retries, queue semantics, and handoff safety for the scheduler.

Validation:
The scheduler design prevents ambiguous ownership and preserves replayability.

issues.title: Design approvals, escalation, and human override
issues.body:
Outcome:
Define the approval graph, escalation rules, and how humans can override flow safely.

Validation:
The system knows exactly when to pause, escalate, or accept explicit human intervention.

issues.title: Design the multi-agent simulation harness
issues.body:
Outcome:
Define the simulation environment and failure-injection scenarios used to evaluate orchestration before full implementation.

Validation:
The harness covers contention, retries, approvals, and deadlock-style failure modes.

issues.title: Define deterministic tool and effect envelopes
issues.body:
Outcome:
Define the canonical envelope for tools and effects so execution remains bounded and replay-aware.

Validation:
Tool usage can be reasoned about without implicit side channels.

issues.title: Design executor boundaries
issues.body:
Outcome:
Define the executor interfaces for HTTP, shell, browser, and internal runtime actions.

Validation:
The execution plane separates intent, policy, and side-effect delivery cleanly.

issues.title: Design the capability policy engine
issues.body:
Outcome:
Define the policy model for capabilities, scopes, approvals, and execution permissions.

Validation:
A reviewer can explain what a task may do, under what authority, and how that is enforced.

issues.title: Design artifact storage and execution attachments
issues.body:
Outcome:
Define how execution artifacts, evidence, and attachments are stored, linked, retained, and surfaced.

Validation:
Artifacts have a clear lifecycle and support replay, review, and memory extraction.

issues.title: Define the MVP tool pack
issues.body:
Outcome:
Define the initial set of tools required for MVP runtime usefulness.

Validation:
The tool pack is small, policy-aware, and sufficient to support the MVP execution loops.

issues.title: Design the immutable execution packet and fail-closed task result
issues.body:
Outcome:
Define the frozen execution packet and the task-result contract that prevents invalid outputs from causing downstream continuation.

Validation:
Task execution cannot proceed on partial, malformed, or ambiguous result payloads.

issues.title: Define memory strata and retention
issues.body:
Outcome:
Define the memory layers, retention windows, and intended uses for each layer.

Validation:
The memory model avoids mixing ephemeral runtime state with durable knowledge.

issues.title: Design memory extraction from runs, artifacts, and GitHub events
issues.body:
Outcome:
Define how durable knowledge is extracted from execution traces, artifacts, and GitHub activity.

Validation:
Knowledge extraction rules are explicit, bounded, and auditable.

issues.title: Design retrieval policy and invalidation
issues.body:
Outcome:
Define what memory can be retrieved, when it is valid, and how stale or invalid memory is rejected.

Validation:
The retrieval model does not smuggle in hidden state or outdated context.

issues.title: Design provenance graph and knowledge digestion
issues.body:
Outcome:
Define the provenance graph and the process that turns raw evidence into reusable knowledge.

Validation:
Each knowledge artifact can be traced back to source evidence and transformation steps.

issues.title: Design recall and contamination evaluation
issues.body:
Outcome:
Define the evaluation suite used to measure recall quality, contamination risk, and knowledge usefulness.

Validation:
The team can tell whether the memory system is helping or harming execution quality.

issues.title: Define the public API surface
issues.body:
Outcome:
Define the public API contracts for companies, agents, objectives, runs, approvals, and related control surfaces.

Validation:
The API pack is coherent and aligned with the domain and GitHub projection models.

issues.title: Design timeline, event stream, and control endpoints
issues.body:
Outcome:
Define the control-plane backend needed for timelines, streams, and operator control actions.

Validation:
An operator can inspect and act on runtime state through explicit backend contracts.

issues.title: Design the base operator UI
issues.body:
Outcome:
Define the main operator experience for viewing companies, agents, runs, approvals, and current state.

Validation:
The base UI can explain runtime state without requiring hidden chat context.

issues.title: Design GitHub-linked progress and drift alerts
issues.body:
Outcome:
Define the progress views and alerts that show how runtime state relates to GitHub projections.

Validation:
Operators can detect projection drift and inspect the reason without guesswork.

issues.title: Design GitHub App onboarding and first company bootstrap
issues.body:
Outcome:
Define the onboarding path for connecting GitHub and bootstrapping the first company workspace.

Validation:
A new operator can understand how to connect a repository and stand up the first company instance.

issues.title: Define SLOs, tracing, and error taxonomy
issues.body:
Outcome:
Define the core service levels, tracing model, and error taxonomy for alpha operations.

Validation:
Readiness work can use a single operational language for health, latency, and failure modes.

issues.title: Enforce replay regression in CI
issues.body:
Outcome:
Define and implement the CI gate that prevents replay regressions from landing unnoticed.

Validation:
Replay-based correctness is a merge requirement rather than a manual hope.

issues.title: Close security review for permissions, secrets, and auditability
issues.body:
Outcome:
Complete the alpha security review focused on permissions, secrets handling, and auditability.

Validation:
Known security assumptions are written down and reviewed before alpha release.

issues.title: Design end-to-end reference workflows
issues.body:
Outcome:
Define the reference workflows used to validate the system end to end during alpha.

Validation:
The team has a small, explicit suite of realistic flows for launch gating.

issues.title: Prepare the alpha release and operational runbook
issues.body:
Outcome:
Prepare the release checklist, rollback guidance, operational runbook, and alpha handoff packet.

Validation:
Alpha can launch with an explicit operational path instead of tribal knowledge.
